title: Boatswain's Mate
description: Operates and maintains boats, deck equipment, and aids to navigation. Leads seamanship operations, search and rescue, and law enforcement boardings.

title: Machinery Technician
description: Operates, maintains, and repairs diesel engines, gas turbines, pumps, and other mechanical systems aboard Coast Guard cutters and boats.

title: Electronics Technician
description: Installs, operates, and maintains electronic navigation, communication, and detection systems including radar, GPS, and radio equipment.

title: Electrician's Mate
description: Maintains and repairs electrical power generation and distribution systems, motors, lighting, and associated electrical equipment on cutters.

title: Information Systems Technician
description: Installs, operates, and maintains computer systems, networks, cybersecurity infrastructure, and information technology equipment.

title: Storekeeper
description: Manages supply chain operations, procurement, inventory control, and financial management for Coast Guard units.

title: Yeoman
description: Performs personnel administration, human resources management, legal assistance, and office management functions.

title: Health Services Technician
description: Provides emergency medical care, preventive medicine, dental assistance, and health services as the primary medical caregiver for Coast Guard units.

title: Maritime Enforcement Specialist
description: Conducts law enforcement boardings, port security operations, drug and migrant interdiction, and use-of-force operations.

title: Gunner's Mate
description: Maintains and operates small arms, crew-served weapons, pyrotechnics, and ordnance systems aboard Coast Guard vessels.

title: Damage Controlman
description: Performs damage control, firefighting, flooding response, and chemical/biological/radiological defense to maintain vessel survivability.

title: Operations Specialist
description: Operates radar, communications, and navigation systems in command centers to coordinate search and rescue, maritime patrol, and traffic management.

title: Aviation Electrical Technician
description: Inspects, troubleshoots, and repairs aircraft avionics, electrical systems, navigation equipment, and communications systems at Coast Guard air stations.

title: Aviation Maintenance Technician
description: Maintains and repairs Coast Guard aircraft airframes, engines, hydraulic systems, and structural components to ensure airworthiness.

title: Aviation Survival Technician
description: Serves as rescue swimmer and paramedic, performing helicopter hoist rescues and emergency medical care in the most demanding search and rescue conditions.

title: Avionics Electrical Technician
description: Maintains and repairs aircraft avionics and electronic systems. Note: This rating was redesignated as AET. Use AET for current requirements.

title: Marine Science Technician
description: Enforces marine environmental laws, responds to oil and chemical spills, conducts port safety inspections, and monitors marine pollution.

title: Public Affairs Specialist
description: Manages media relations, produces multimedia content, coordinates public information campaigns, and serves as the Coast Guard's communications representative.

title: Culinary Specialist
description: Prepares and serves meals, manages food service operations, and maintains galleys and dining facilities aboard cutters and at shore installations.

title: Intelligence Specialist
description: Collects, analyzes, and disseminates intelligence to support Coast Guard operations including counter-narcotics, counter-terrorism, and maritime security.

title: Investigator
description: Conducts criminal investigations, fraud inquiries, and administrative investigations within the Coast Guard Investigative Service (CGIS).

title: Port Security Specialist
description: Conducts port and waterways security operations, vessel and facility inspections, and maritime homeland security missions.
